demos.text: In general, we advise you to try and keep the topology (the layout) of your DAG tasks relatively stable; dynamic DAGs are usually better used for dynamically loading configuration options or changing operator options.
demos.reasoning: The passage is concise, domain-specific guidance with natural technical phrasing and a documentation-like style. It does not show strong signs of AI-generated text, so it is more likely human-authored.

demos.text: In general, try to keep the topology (that is, the layout) of your DAG tasks reasonably stable; dynamic DAGs tend to work better for loading configuration values dynamically or adjusting operator options as needed.
demos.reasoning: The passage is concise and polished, with a generalized instructional tone and explicit parenthetical clarification, which are common characteristics of AI-generated technical prose. Its wording also feels somewhat formulaic and lacks context or personal specificity. Therefore, it is likely AI-generated.
